segments.beat: extreme close-up of device face-up in hands, blue-lit see-through display active, PCB internals visible through clear shell
device rotated and tilted, showing transparent side profile and top edge; internals remain visible through resin shell
continued rotation revealing all faces of the clear shell; USB-C port and PCB components visible through walls
device held up toward camera at face level; display lit with checkerboard/game pattern; transparent shell catches ambient light
wide pull-back to hand-held hero shot; full device scale relative to palm confirmed; display dims slightly; final hold

demonstrates.motion_value: continuous rotation reveals all four transparent walls sequentially — no single still captures the full wrap-around PCB visibility
live blue pixel grid on display persists across rotation, proving it is a functional lit transparent pane rather than a printed graphic
device dwarfed by palm in pull-back confirms sub-100mm form factor in three dimensions
rotation catches specular highlights on polished resin edges, conveying premium finish that a flat still undersells
slow deliberate hold at face level mimics handling a display object; ambient light interaction confirms aesthetic intent
checkerboard pixel pattern on display is consistent with retro game content but no controller interaction shown

selection_note: Best single-asset proof of the two load-bearing claims — transparency and live see-through display — captured simultaneously across a clean 28-second rotation; cuts cleanly at any point for a loopable hero.

aesthetics.standout: vivid cyan display glow in close-ups
aesthetics.weakness: harsh diagonal shadows, cluttered tile background, handheld shake, face intrudes mid-clip
aesthetics.note: opens with decent tabletop close-ups then degrades into uncontrolled handheld against a tile wall with window shadow bars - full reshoot needed
